facts.text: Jewett City was named after industrialist Eliezer Jewett, who founded the settlement in 1771 and built the first sawmill and gristmill on the Pachaug River.
The borough was officially incorporated in 1895, becoming the first incorporated borough in Connecticut.
In 2007, the town’s historic church bells sparked a national comedy segment on The Daily Show, bringing unexpected media attention.
Jewett City sits within the town of Griswold in New London County, part of the southeastern Connecticut Planning Region.
The community’s early economy relied on agriculture until the late 1700s, when a fulling mill began producing homemade flannel for clothing.
The Pachaug River’s water power was harnessed early on, making Jewett City one of Connecticut’s first mill towns.
